docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: The Open Energy Ontology (OEO) is a domain ontology of the energy system analysis context. It is developed as part of the Open Energy Family. The OEO is published on GitHub under an open source license. The language used is the Manchester OWL Syntax, which was chosen because it is user-friendly for editing and viewing differences of edited files. The OEO is constantly being extended. The first version of the OEO has been released on June 11th 2020. A Steering Committee (OEO-SC) was created to accompany the development, increase awareness of the ontology and include it in current projects.

docs.title: Mission
docs.text: The mission is to establish a long-term organization that supports and guides both ontology developers and ontology users. It serves as a central point of contact for all interested stakeholders.

docs.title: Get Started
docs.text:
❓ Are you an ontology developer? ▶ Start here to learn how to contribute, align with the principles, and join the community.

❓ Are you an ontology user? ▶ Start here to explore available ontologies and learn how to apply them in your work.

❓ New to ontologies? ▶ Start here for an introduction to the basics and how ontologies can support your work.

docs.title: GitHub Issues
docs.text: Technical improvements and new features are documented and discussed in GitHub issues: 🌍 github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues

docs.title: Keywords
docs.text: climate, policy, energy policy, climate policy atlas, CEPO, NFDI4Energy, ontology, sustainability, legislation, targets, policy instruments

docs.title: Challenges
docs.text: Researchers examining policy instrument data related to energy and the climate need a standardized way to code data from text documents, so that the data is machine-readable and easily comparable to other data.

docs.title: Solutions
docs.text: The Climate and Energy Policy Ontology (CEPO) catalogs terminology that describes policy instruments and targets related to sustainable energy transition. It forms the semantic base for the Climate Policy Atlas, a web portal for exploring energy-related policy data. Researchers use CEPO to annotate policy documents, improving data interoperability and helping to highlight connections between data.

docs.title: OEO Connection
docs.text:
• CEPO’s entity structure and development workflows are based on those of the OEO.
  • CEPO was designed for interoperability with the OEO, using BFO and some OEO terms as a base for initial development.
  • Like the OEO, CEPO follows the ENERO Foundry principles.
